text: Commemorating the 50th anniversary of the Mizar project, we present a high-performance AI/TP system capable of automatically proving approximately 60% of Mizar theorems in a standard hammer setting. When the automated provers are restricted to the specific premises utilized in human-authored proofs, this success rate increases to 75%. We detail the methodologies and large-scale experimental frameworks that underpin these results, highlighting the roles of the E and Vampire theorem provers, along with their specialized neural extensions, ENIGMA and Deepire. A key component of our approach is an incremental training loop that alternates between generating a corpus of millions of ATP proofs and refining the AI models based on this data. Finally, we showcase a selection of challenging Mizar problems that are now solvable through full automation.
